title: eragrostis cilianensis var. leersioides
intro: Eragrostis cilianensis var. leersioides, also known as squirrel tail grass or wild rye, is a type of perennial grass native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting respiratory health. Eragrostis cilianensis var. leersioides is often prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses.
